Train the decoder from random initialization on the packed corpus, co-training instruction-following by mixing rendered Question/Answer pairs into the corpus; single-GPU now, multi-GPU ready.

Stage three. The from-scratch decoder learns next-token prediction on the packed corpus, which includes generated instruction pairs rendered as light Question/Answer text and mixed in at pretrain.instruction_fraction of tokens, with a cosine schedule, gradient accumulation, mixed precision, and checkpointing.

Pretraining is the core language-competence step and, since the SFT redesign, the primary place instruction-following is learned. Instruction pairs are upsampled or downsampled to hit the configured token fraction and mixed into the same packed corpus as ordinary documents, so the model learns to follow the Question/Answer format jointly with fluency rather than through a separate, distribution-shifting phase (see node 30 for why this matters and node 39 for the mechanistic explanation via circuit formation). It runs on a single L40S by default and supports distributed data parallel for multi-GPU scaling, tracks validation loss with early stopping, saves best and last checkpoints, resumes from the last checkpoint, and logs tokens per non-embedding parameter. Effective batch size, schedule, and model preset are configurable.
